artifacts.title: About Common Cold
assertions: Common-cold symptom cluster may include runny or congested nose, cough, sneezing, sore throat, headache, mild body aches and usually low-grade fever in older children and adults.

artifacts.title: Cough (acute): antimicrobial prescribing
assertions: Acute cough is commonly self-limiting and often related to viral upper-respiratory infection.
Rapid or significant worsening, systemic severe illness, or signs of serious illness require reassessment or referral.
Immunosuppression and significant comorbidity can increase complication risk.

artifacts.title: ERS guidelines on diagnosis and treatment of chronic cough in adults and children
assertions: Chronic-cough history should cover onset, time course, nature, associated symptoms, triggers, day/night variation and irritant exposure.
Assessment should consider ACE-inhibitor exposure and common treatable traits while preserving uncertainty.
Potential warning features include chest pain, dyspnea, hemoptysis, recurrent infection, immunodeficiency and tuberculosis exposure risk.

artifacts.title: 2025 GINA Summary Guide
assertions: Asthma-like symptom patterns may vary and may occur with wheeze, breathing difficulty, exercise association or night symptoms.
Alternative causes and medication-related cough should remain distinct considerations.
notes: GINA states that a 2026 Summary Guide is forthcoming; monitor early.

artifacts.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults
assertions: Chronic-cough history should identify underlying disease, treatable traits and referral red flags.
Productive cough, recurrent infection, hemoptysis and malignancy-related warning features require targeted assessment rather than diagnostic assumption.

limitations: Only source metadata and narrowly paraphrased assertions are stored; full source artifacts are not cached.
Licensing and clinical interpretation require review.
The knowledge derived from this manifest is restricted to research and simulation.
